pi_items.source: setup_task.sh (line 22) and export_result.sh (line 9) - file path where diagram is to be saved
export_result.sh (line 10) - file path where PNG export is to be saved
setup_task.sh (line 26) - file path where synthetic specification is created

RFC 6749 (OAuth 2.0 Authorization Framework) defines four roles: Resource Owner (called 'User' in task), Client, Authorization Server, Resource Server. All four are present in the synthetic specification.

RFC 6749 (OAuth 2.0 Authorization Framework) and RFC 7636 (Proof Key for Code Exchange / PKCE). All keywords are authentic protocol terms verified in both RFCs and present in the synthetic specification.
Task description and synthetic specification both define two pages: Page 1 (Authorization Code Flow with PKCE) and Page 2 (Token Refresh Flow)
Synthetic specification PROTOCOL FLOW section describes 10 distinct message exchanges (including 2 self-messages). Requirement of min 8 messages is exceeded.

privileged_info_summary: The task is based on authentic OAuth 2.0 and PKCE specifications (RFC 6749 and RFC 7636). The specification document is synthetically created by task setup but accurately reflects the authorization code flow with PKCE. The specification defines 4 participants (User/Resource Owner, Client, Authorization Server, Resource Server) and describes 10 message exchanges across 2 pages. All required keywords (code_challenge, code_verifier, access_token, refresh_token, bearer, authorization code) are authentic OAuth 2.0 and PKCE protocol terms verified in official RFCs. The file paths for expected outputs are consistent across all task scripts.
